sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Marketo connector syncs leads, static lists, campaigns, and programs from Marketo (Adobe) into the Shielva knowledge base using the Marketo REST API. Authentication uses OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials via a LaunchPoint Custom service — no user login required. The connector implements nextPageToken-based pagination for leads/lists/campaigns, offset-based pagination for programs, automatic token refresh, exponential backoff retry, and Marketo-specific success:false error body handling.

subsections.title: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials
subsections.content: Marketo uses OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials (not Authorization Code). The connector exchanges client_id + client_secret for a Bearer token at https://{munchkin_id}.mktorest.com/identity/oauth/token. Tokens expire in 3600 seconds and are refreshed automatically with a 60-second buffer before expiry.
steps: Log in to Marketo Admin → LaunchPoint → New Service (Custom).
Assign an API-only user with Access API role permissions.
Copy Client ID and Client Secret from View Details.
Find your Munchkin ID at Admin → Munchkin.
Enter all three values in the Shielva connector install form.

fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The Client ID from your Marketo LaunchPoint Custom service (Admin → LaunchPoint → View Details).

fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The Client Secret from your Marketo LaunchPoint Custom service.

fields.label: Munchkin ID
fields.description: Your Marketo instance identifier (Admin → Munchkin). Format: abc-123-xyz.

resources.title: Leads
resources.description: Marketo leads (contacts/prospects). Paginated via nextPageToken. Source URL: https://app.marketo.com/#/lead/{id}.

resources.title: Static Lists
resources.description: Marketo static lists — groupings of leads. Paginated via nextPageToken.

resources.title: Campaigns
resources.description: Marketo smart and batch campaigns. Types include batch and trigger. Paginated via nextPageToken.

resources.title: Programs
resources.description: Marketo programs (email, event, webinar, engagement, etc.). Paginated via offset.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content: Marketo always returns HTTP 200 even for errors — real errors are in the body as {"success": false, "errors": [{"code": "601", "message": "..."}]}. The connector checks the success field on every response. Error codes 600/601/602/603 map to MarketoAuthError; 606 maps to MarketoRateLimitError; 702 maps to MarketoNotFoundError. HTTP 429 also triggers MarketoRateLimitError with the Retry-After header honored. All non-auth errors are retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff.

sections.title: Rate Limits
sections.content: Marketo REST API has a default limit of 10,000 API calls per day. Burst limits apply. The connector retries on error code 606 with exponential backoff. For high-volume accounts, request a daily limit increase from Marketo support.

methods.name: Install
methods.description: Validates client_id, client_secret, and munchkin_id by obtaining an access token and probing the leads endpoint.

methods.name: Health Check
methods.description: Probes the Marketo leads endpoint and returns HEALTHY, DEGRADED, or OFFLINE.

methods.name: Sync
methods.description: Full paginated sync of leads, lists, campaigns, and programs into the knowledge base.

methods.name: List Leads
methods.description: Returns all leads as a list of raw dicts.

methods.name: Get Lead
methods.description: Retrieves a single lead by integer ID.

items.fix: Re-copy all three values from Marketo Admin. Ensure the LaunchPoint service is active.

items.cause: API-only user lacks Access API role permission
items.fix: Add Access API permission to the user's role in Admin → Users & Roles.
Reduce sync frequency. Request a daily limit increase from Marketo support.

items.cause: Transient network error or Marketo service disruption
items.fix: Check https://status.adobe.com for Marketo service status. Retry after a few minutes.
